description: Cross-paper edges of the PFR proof dependency graph. Each edge connects theorem-level node IDs across papers (or to a declared foundational anchor). Direction reads subject-verb-object: '{from} {type} {to}'.

edges.note: The PFR fibring lemma (Prop 4.1) is the fibring inequality Prop 1.4 of Green-Manners-Tao, the tool that enables the entropy induction.
The entropic PFR proof uses the very-small-entropy-doubling characterization (Lemma 5.1) from Green-Manners-Tao.
The endgame Lemma 7.2 uses the entropic Balog-Szemeredi-Gowers lemma from Tao's 2010 entropy sumset theory.
Green-Manners-Tao's Prop 1.10 is conditional on PFR over F_2; once GGMT proved Conjecture 1.1 the implication became unconditional.
The GGMT main theorem, via Green-Manners-Tao Prop 1.10, settles weak PFR over the integers (Conjecture 1.7 of that paper).
Liao improves the covering exponent of the main PFR theorem from C=12 to C=9.
Liao improves the entropic PFR constant from 11 to 8.
Liao's sharpened tau-functional decrease lemma tightens the GGMT core induction step.
Bloom extends the entropic PFR framework from F_2^n to function fields F_q[t].
Bloom's vector-space dimension characterization uses the Green-Manners-Tao fibring inequality.
Abbe-Sandon-Shashkov-Viazovska restate GGMT entropic PFR as their Theorem 14.
The conditional entropic PFR corollary extends GGMT entropic PFR to a conditioned setting.
The Reed-Muller capacity theorem relies on entropic PFR through the orbit-localization chain.
Lau-Nair generalize the Ruzsa equivalence underlying the entropy-combinatorics basics of GGMT Appendix A.
Lau-Nair give the entropic version of Ruzsa's classical sum-difference inequality.
Raghavan's bound approaches the PFR conjecture for general abelian groups (any epsilon>0) without reaching epsilon=0.
Raghavan adapts the GGMT tau-functional framework, used in Prop 2.1, to general abelian groups.
Raghavan's fibring lemma is the Green-Manners-Tao fibring inequality applied to general groups.
Raghavan cites GGMT Propositions 2.1 and 2.2 (the tau-decrement) as a foundation.
The two-source disperser-to-extractor upgrade combines Ben-Aroya-Regev with GGMT entropic PFR.
The low-error two-source extractor uses entropic PFR via the Theorem 9 disperser-to-extractor conversion.
The m-torsion covering theorem cited by Karam generalizes the GGMT F_2 covering theorem.
Karam uses the m-torsion form of GGMT entropic PFR.
Karam uses Green-Manners-Tao Prop 1.3: small Ruzsa distance implies closeness to a subgroup.
Karam's distributional characterization of small symmetric differences with cosets uses entropic PFR.
The Z^D rank lemma is a direct application of GGMT weak PFR over the integers.
The doubling-to-rank lemma uses weak PFR over Z, now a theorem via the GGMT proof chain.
Harrison-Mudgal-Schmidt's resolution of Bremner's conjecture rests, through the rank lemmas, on weak PFR over the integers.
